items.title: Litton ruled out of Zimbabwe Test
items.summary: Bangladesh wicketkeeper-batter Litton Das has been ruled out of the one-off Test against Zimbabwe, scheduled to begin on June 28 in Harare, the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) announced in a press release on Sunday.

items.title: Litton ruled out of Zimbabwe Test with calf injury
items.summary: The keeper-batter, who also missed the recently concluded three-match T20I series against Australia in Chattogram, has been replaced in the squad by Mahidul Islam Bhuiyan

items.title: Mahidul Islam replaces injured Litton Das for Zimbabwe Test
items.summary: Litton Das hasn't sufficiently recovered from the calf strain

items.title: Simmons 'disappointed' by Australia whitewash
items.summary: The Bangladesh head coach said that the batting unit let the side down in the 3-0 series defeat

items.title: Bowlers, Marsh help Australia complete T20I series sweep over Bangladesh
items.summary: Towhid Hridoy's unbeaten 61 proved insufficient as Mitchell Marsh's 28-ball 60 powered Australia to a seven-wicket win and 3-0 sweep

items.title: Bangladesh bat first, make three changes in search of consolation win
items.summary: Australia named an unchanged side as they look to whitewash the T20 series

items.title: Australia romp to 3-0 series whitewash
items.summary: Captain Mitchell Marsh stroked a 28-ball 60 as Australia romped to a 3-0 series victory over Bangladesh with a seven-wicket thrashing in the third and final T20I in Chattogram on Sunday.

items.title: Pakistan 'defeated ourselves' against Bangladesh - Wahab
items.summary: Team mentor laments disastrous batting collapse after being 70 for 2 chasing 124

items.title: Shorna, spinners eliminate Pakistan from 2026 T20 World Cup
items.summary: After Shorna's cameo propelled Bangladesh, Nahida and Meghla took three wickets to help defend 123

items.title: Tigresses beat Pakistan to keep semifinal hopes alive
items.summary: Shorna Akter played a brilliant late cameo before spinners Nahida Akter and Shanjida Akter Meghla claimed three wickets apiece as Bangladesh secured a crucial 23-run victory over Pakistan in their third Group A match of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup at The Rose Bowl in Southampton on Saturday.

items.title: Bangladesh bat; Pakistan bring back Rubab, Jabeen
items.summary: Pakistan are in must-win territory after losing to India and South Africa

items.title: Misfiring Bangladesh look for lift as Australia eye series sweep
items.summary: Taskin Ahmed could replace the off-colour Rishad Hossain for the final game of the series, while Australia might be tempted to give Josh Philippe and Matt Kuhnemann a game

items.title: Tigresses eye winning return against Pakistan
items.summary: Bangladesh will draw confidence from their recent success against Pakistan in white-ball cricket when they face their South Asian rivals in a crucial Women's T20 World Cup on Saturday.

items.title: ‘We were progressing’: Emon rues momentum shift as Australia win T20I series
items.summary: Before the series, Bangladesh have been undergoing an upsurge in T20Is, losing just one of their last seven series and drawing another.

items.title: Australia bowlers close out T20I series after Renshaw stars with 89*
items.summary: Bangladesh were well placed in the chase but couldn't quite get over the line as the visitors finished well with the ball

items.title: Australia bat first and recall Hardie in bid to wrap up T20I series
items.summary: Bangladesh have made two changes, bringing in Nahid Rana and left-arm spinner Nasum Ahmed

items.title: Bangladesh face bowling conundrum as Australia look to seal series
items.summary: Bangladesh also have questions regarding their middle order as they look to stay alive in the series

items.title: Debutant Davies lives his dream on banner day for Australia's spinners
items.summary: Joel Davies took 3 for 17 as Australia's spinners sank Bangladesh in the first T20I in Chattogram
